stories.title: Create Collection
stories.spec:
Acceptance Criteria:
- User can open a 'New Collection' dialog/input and submit a name
- Collection is saved to SQLite with fields: id (UUID), name, created_at
- Collection appears immediately in the sidebar list after creation
- Empty name is rejected with inline validation

Technical Approach:
- SQLite table: collections(id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, created_at DATETIME)
- Wails-bound Go method: CreateCollection(name string) (Collection, error)
- Frontend calls binding, updates local state on success

Files likely affected:
- main.go (Wails app setup)
- app.go (Wails App struct, CreateCollection binding)
- db/db.go (SQLite init, migrations)
- db/collections.go (CRUD queries)
- frontend/src/components/Sidebar.tsx
- frontend/src/components/NewCollectionModal.tsx
- frontend/src/store/collectionsStore.ts

stories.title: Rename Collection
stories.spec:
Acceptance Criteria:
- User can double-click or right-click a collection to rename it
- New name is persisted in SQLite
- Sidebar updates immediately
- Empty name is rejected

Technical Approach:
- Wails-bound Go method: RenameCollection(id string, name string) error
- UPDATE query on collections table
- Frontend inline edit or modal

Files likely affected:
- app.go
- db/collections.go
- frontend/src/components/Sidebar.tsx
- frontend/src/components/CollectionItem.tsx

stories.title: Delete Collection
stories.spec:
Acceptance Criteria:
- User can delete a collection via right-click menu or button
- Confirmation prompt shown before deletion
- Collection and all its child requests are removed from SQLite (cascade)
- Sidebar updates immediately

Technical Approach:
- Wails-bound Go method: DeleteCollection(id string) error
- SQLite: DELETE FROM collections WHERE id=? (requests table has ON DELETE CASCADE foreign key)
- Frontend removes collection and clears active request if it belonged to deleted collection

Files likely affected:
- app.go
- db/collections.go
- db/requests.go
- frontend/src/components/Sidebar.tsx
- frontend/src/components/CollectionItem.tsx
- frontend/src/store/collectionsStore.ts

stories.title: Create Request
stories.spec:
Acceptance Criteria:
- User can add a new request inside a collection
- Request is saved to SQLite with default values: method=GET, url='', headers=[], params=[], body_type='none', body=''
- New request appears in the collection's request list in the sidebar
- User can name the request

Technical Approach:
- SQLite table: requests(id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, collection_id TEXT REFERENCES collections(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, name TEXT, method TEXT, url TEXT, headers TEXT, params TEXT, body_type TEXT, body TEXT, created_at DATETIME)
- headers and params stored as JSON strings
- Wails-bound Go method: CreateRequest(collectionId string, name string) (Request, error)

Files likely affected:
- app.go
- db/requests.go
- frontend/src/components/Sidebar.tsx
- frontend/src/components/CollectionItem.tsx
- frontend/src/store/requestsStore.ts

stories.title: Configure HTTP Method
stories.spec:
Acceptance Criteria:
- User sees a method selector (dropdown) in the request editor: GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE
- Selected method is saved to SQLite when changed
- Method persists when re-opening the request

Technical Approach:
- Wails-bound Go method: UpdateRequest(req RequestPayload) error (shared update method for all request fields)
- Frontend dropdown updates local state and calls UpdateRequest on change

Files likely affected:
- app.go
- db/requests.go
- frontend/src/components/RequestEditor.tsx
- frontend/src/components/MethodSelector.tsx

stories.title: Configure URL
stories.spec:
Acceptance Criteria:
- User can type a URL in a text input in the request editor
- URL is saved to SQLite (debounced or on blur)
- URL persists when re-opening the request

Technical Approach:
- Part of UpdateRequest binding
- Frontend text input with debounce (300ms) or on-blur save

Files likely affected:
- frontend/src/components/RequestEditor.tsx
- frontend/src/components/UrlBar.tsx

stories.title: Configure Headers
stories.spec:
Acceptance Criteria:
- User can add, edit, and remove key-value header pairs in a table within the request editor
- Headers are saved to SQLite as a JSON array [{key, value, enabled}]
- Headers persist when re-opening the request
- Rows can be disabled (checkbox) without deletion

Technical Approach:
- Stored in requests.headers as JSON string
- UpdateRequest binding serializes headers array
- Frontend renders a KeyValueTable component reused for headers and query params

Files likely affected:
- app.go
- db/requests.go
- frontend/src/components/RequestEditor.tsx
- frontend/src/components/KeyValueTable.tsx

stories.title: Configure Query Parameters
stories.spec:
Acceptance Criteria:
- User can add, edit, and remove key-value query param pairs
- Params are saved to SQLite as a JSON array [{key, value, enabled}]
- Enabled params are appended to the URL as ?key=value&... when request is sent
- Params persist when re-opening the request

Technical Approach:
- Stored in requests.params as JSON string
- Go backend builds final URL by appending enabled params before execution
- Reuses KeyValueTable component from US-7

Files likely affected:
- app.go
- db/requests.go
- frontend/src/components/RequestEditor.tsx
- frontend/src/components/KeyValueTable.tsx

stories.title: Configure Request Body
stories.spec:
Acceptance Criteria:
- User can select body type: None, JSON, Raw, Form-data
- For JSON and Raw: a textarea/code editor is shown
- For Form-data: a key-value table is shown (same KeyValueTable pattern)
- Body content and type are saved to SQLite
- Body persists when re-opening the request
- Body tab/section is disabled or hidden for GET/DELETE (or shown with a warning)

Technical Approach:
- body_type: 'none' | 'json' | 'raw' | 'form-data'
- body: TEXT (JSON string for form-data rows, raw string otherwise)
- UpdateRequest binding handles body fields
- Go backend sets Content-Type header automatically based on body_type (application/json, text/plain, multipart/form-data)

Files likely affected:
- app.go
- db/requests.go
- frontend/src/components/RequestEditor.tsx
- frontend/src/components/BodyEditor.tsx
- frontend/src/components/KeyValueTable.tsx

stories.title: Send Request
stories.spec:
Acceptance Criteria:
- User clicks a Send button in the request editor
- The Go backend executes the HTTP request using net/http
- Headers (enabled only), query params (enabled only), and body are applied
- A loading/spinner state is shown while request is in-flight
- Result (status, body, headers, duration, size) is returned to the frontend
- Errors (timeout, connection refused, invalid URL) are surfaced to the user

Technical Approach:
- Wails-bound Go method: SendRequest(id string) (ResponseResult, error)
- ResponseResult struct: { StatusCode int, StatusText string, Body string, Headers map[string][]string, DurationMs int64, SizeBytes int64 }
- net/http client with a reasonable timeout (30s default)
- Measure duration with time.Now() / time.Since()
- Measure size from len(body bytes)
- Frontend stores ResponseResult in state for the response viewer

Files likely affected:
- app.go
- http/client.go (new file for HTTP execution logic)
- frontend/src/components/RequestEditor.tsx
- frontend/src/store/responseStore.ts

stories.title: View Response Status
stories.spec:
Acceptance Criteria:
- After Send, the response panel shows the HTTP status code (e.g. 200, 404, 500)
- Status text is shown alongside code (e.g. '200 OK')
- Status is color-coded: 2xx green, 3xx blue, 4xx orange, 5xx red

Technical Approach:
- Reads from ResponseResult.StatusCode and ResponseResult.StatusText in frontend state
- StatusBadge component with conditional color class

Files likely affected:
- frontend/src/components/ResponseViewer.tsx
- frontend/src/components/StatusBadge.tsx

stories.title: View Response Body
stories.spec:
Acceptance Criteria:
- Response panel shows the full response body
- If Content-Type is application/json, body is pretty-printed with syntax highlighting
- Raw view always available as a fallback
- Body is scrollable for large responses

Technical Approach:
- Reads ResponseResult.Body (string)
- Use a lightweight syntax highlighter (e.g. Prism.js or a Monaco-lite) or simple <pre> with JSON.stringify(JSON.parse()) pretty print
- Tab toggle: Pretty / Raw

Files likely affected:
- frontend/src/components/ResponseViewer.tsx
- frontend/src/components/ResponseBody.tsx

stories.title: View Response Headers
stories.spec:
Acceptance Criteria:
- Response panel has a Headers tab showing all response headers as key-value pairs
- Headers are readable and scrollable

Technical Approach:
- Reads ResponseResult.Headers (map[string][]string)
- Renders as a read-only table

Files likely affected:
- frontend/src/components/ResponseViewer.tsx
- frontend/src/components/ResponseHeaders.tsx

stories.title: View Request Metrics
stories.spec:
Acceptance Criteria:
- After Send, the UI shows response time in milliseconds (e.g. '142 ms')
- UI shows response size in human-readable form (e.g. '1.2 KB' or '340 B')
- Metrics are displayed in the response panel header/toolbar area

Technical Approach:
- Reads ResponseResult.DurationMs and ResponseResult.SizeBytes
- Frontend formats size: if < 1024 show bytes, else show KB with 1 decimal
- Displayed inline next to status badge

Files likely affected:
- frontend/src/components/ResponseViewer.tsx
- frontend/src/components/MetricsBar.tsx
